title: dactylorhiza latifolia var. longifolia
intro: Dactylorhiza latifolia var. longifolia, commonly known as the broad-leaved marsh orchid, is a rare and endangered species of orchid native to Europe and Western Asia. This perennial plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects. The health benefits of Dactylorhiza latifolia var. longifolia include its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are typically dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
